title: Green Scared? Some Lessons from the FBI Crackdown on Eco-Activists
description: Analysis of Operation Backfire, the FBI's 2005-2006 crackdown on ELF / ALF activists, including how the federal cases unfolded and what they teach. Directly addresses Daniel McGowan, Jonathan Paul, Stanislas Meyerhoff, Chelsea Gerlach, Briana Waters, and other Green Scare prisoners.

title: The Secret World of Terijian
description: Illustrated children's-book-style zine reissued as a fundraising and solidarity tool for June 11 — the international day of solidarity with long-term anarchist prisoners. Proceeds and proceeds-of-distribution support Marius Mason, Eric McDavid, and other long-term anarchist political prisoners.

title: How to Survive a Felony Trial
description: First-person practical guide to navigating a felony prosecution as a political defendant. Used widely across political-prisoner support networks as a pre-prison briefing for new defendants.

title: What Is Security Culture?
description: Foundational guide to operational security practices designed to prevent state repression and protect movement participants. Cornerstone anti-repression text repeatedly cited by Green Scare, NATO 3, Cleveland 4, and J20 defendants in their support materials.

title: Make Ready: Safeguarding Our Movements against Repression
description: Guide to preparing social movements for state repression and terrorism designations under the second Trump administration. Explicitly addressed to people likely to be targeted as political prisoners.

title: Anarchist Resistance to the Trump Inauguration
description: Account of the January 20, 2017 mobilization in Washington, D.C. and the events that set the stage for the J20 mass prosecution of 230+ people on felony rioting charges.

title: We've Got Your Back: The Story of the J20 Defense
description: 32-page history of the collective defense campaign that defeated the J20 mass conspiracy prosecution against more than 230 inauguration-day defendants. Core J20 defendant-support text and case study in modern PP defense organizing.

title: I Was a J20 Street Medic and Defendant
description: First-person narrative from a J20 defendant who also worked as a street medic, on surviving the first trial bloc of the J20 mass prosecution.

title: Load Every Rift With Ore: Critical Reflections on the J20 Trial and Support Campaign
description: 40-page critical retrospective on what the J20 support campaign got right and wrong — lessons-learned document for future political-prisoner support campaigns.

title: Between the Sun and the Sea: Icarus at 12th and L
description: Short reflective piece on a specific J20 defendant and moment from the inauguration-day arrests.

title: Fighting Border Violence, From Obama to Trump
description: Interview with a No More Deaths volunteer who faced federal charges for leaving water in the desert for migrants. Covers the federal prosecution of humanitarian-aid defendants including Scott Warren.

title: Atlanta Police and Georgia State Patrol Are Guilty of Murder
description: Uses the DeKalb County autopsy of Manuel 'Tortuguita' Teran to debunk the police narrative around the January 18, 2023 killing of the forest defender at the Stop Cop City encampment. Foundational document for the Stop Cop City defendants.

title: Understanding the RICO Charges in Atlanta
description: Breakdown of Georgia's sweeping RICO indictment against 61 Stop Cop City activists — the primary explainer for the largest current US anarchist political-prisoner case.

title: Don't Stop: Continuing the Fight against Cop City
description: Six-month account of the Stop Cop City movement from the June 2023 Atlanta City Council vote through the November 2023 Block Cop City convergence — movement context for the Atlanta RICO defendants.
